history.notes: TikTok 'protein girl' trend 100M+ views. Simple protein gram tracker — no dedicated minimal protein-only tracker on App Store. ASO: popularity 38, difficulty 28.
Moved to researching — spawning validator agent

GO: Strong demand signals (3.1M r/xxfitness members, 61% Americans increasing protein intake, TikTok 100M+ views on protein content) with medium monetization potential and near-zero technical risk. Protein-only niche has <10 strong competitors with no dominant winner — differentiation via TikTok-native design and content-driven growth.

demandSignals: TikTok 'protein girl' and 'high protein day of eating' trends with 100M+ combined views
r/xxfitness subreddit has 3.1M members with frequent protein tracking discussions
61% of Americans increased protein intake in 2024 (up from 48% in 2019, per ShelfTrend)
30-30-30 rule (protein-focused morning routine) surged 175% in search interest on TikTok
Multiple listicles ranking 'best protein tracker apps' indicate active search demand (CalAI, WildSociety, Wondershare all published guides in 2025-2026)
marketSize.notes: Protein awareness is in a sustained growth phase, not a spike. The trend spans TikTok fitness content, Reddit communities, and mainstream nutrition media. The target demographic (women 18-35) is especially active in protein-focused fitness content on TikTok.

competitors.identifiedGap: No protein tracker is specifically designed for the TikTok 'protein girl' audience with social-sharing features, streak gamification, and aesthetic design targeting women 18-35. Existing apps are functional but utilitarian. Users on Reddit note that most calorie/macro apps are 'too complex and daunting' — there is room for a more opinionated, simpler experience with better visual design and content tie-ins.
competitors.notes: AppsHunter lists 241+ 'protein tracker' apps broadly, but the vast majority are full macro/calorie trackers. Dedicated protein-only apps number under 10 with meaningful ratings. No single app dominates the niche — the top app (Protein Pal) has ~1500 ratings, leaving room for a well-marketed newcomer. Key competitor risk: Protein Tracker by Kai is completely free, which puts pressure on freemium conversion.

monetization.mrrMath: Conservative: 800 downloads/mo (content-driven) * 3% conversion * $1.99/week * 4.3 weeks = ~$206/mo initially. With TikTok content flywheel scaling to 3000 downloads/mo at 4% conversion: 120 subscribers * $8.56/mo = ~$1,027/mo by month 3. Aggressive (viral content hit): 8000 downloads/mo * 5% = 400 subs * $8.56 = $3,424/mo. Realistic range: $500-$2000 MRR in 3 months.
monetization.notes: The $1.99/week price point ($8.56/mo effective) is competitive — cheaper than macro trackers like MacroFactor ($11.99/mo) but in line with simpler protein-only apps. Free tier must offer enough value to drive retention while gating widgets, analytics, and meal templates behind paywall. Risk: one top competitor is completely free, which may anchor user expectations.

technicalFeasibility.notes: Pure SwiftUI app with local data storage (SwiftData or Core Data). Features: meal protein logging with manual entry, daily goal progress ring, streak counter, simple history view. Optional HealthKit integration for exporting data. No backend required for MVP — all local storage. No expensive APIs needed. Widgets via WidgetKit are straightforward. This is one of the simplest possible app builds.

concerns: Must not make medical/health claims (e.g., 'helps you lose weight' or 'builds muscle') — stick to 'track your protein intake'
HealthKit data must not be stored in iCloud or used for advertising per Apple guidelines
Must include standard health disclaimer if providing any nutritional guidance
appStoreRisk.notes: Multiple protein tracker apps are already approved and live on the App Store, confirming this category is safe. Simple food/nutrient logging does not fall under Guideline 5.1.1 medical app scrutiny as long as we avoid diagnostic or therapeutic claims. No camera, location, or contacts permissions needed for MVP.
